title: Master's degree. Couldn't call the dentist for 3 years.
body:
Nobody ever taught me how to make a dentist appointment.

I know how that sounds. I'm 31. I have a master's degree. I can derive a regression equation and explain the French Revolution in three languages. But last Tuesday I sat in front of my laptop for forty minutes, browser open to a dental practice website, genuinely paralyzed by the question of whether I call them or whether they call me. Whether I need a referral. What I'm supposed to say when someone picks up. Whether "I haven't been in eight years" is something you admit out loud or something you lie about.

I closed the tab. Made tea. Told myself I'd do it tomorrow.

I've been telling myself I'll do it tomorrow for three years.
